metadata.title: Vehicle Speed Control — System Requirements Specification
metadata.author: Systems Engineering Team

requirements.title: Speed control steady-state accuracy
requirements.text: The vehicle speed control system SHALL maintain vehicle speed within ±2 km/h of the driver-set target speed under steady-state conditions on a flat road surface.
requirements.rationale: Deviation beyond ±2 km/h at steady state creates unacceptable speed deviation risk on motorways and may trigger unintended braking in following vehicles.

requirements.title: Brake pedal override deactivation
requirements.text: The vehicle speed control system SHALL immediately deactivate speed control and release all actuator commands within 50 ms of brake pedal depression exceeding 5% of full travel, regardless of current system state.
requirements.rationale: Brake override priority is a fundamental safety invariant. Failure to deactivate on brake input constitutes a hazardous loss of driver control. The 50 ms response is derived from HARA HA-VSC-003.

requirements.title: Vehicle speed sensor CAN interface
requirements.text: The vehicle speed control system SHALL receive vehicle speed data from the wheel speed sensors via CAN FD bus at a minimum rate of 10 ms cycle time. The system SHALL accept speed values encoded as unsigned 16-bit integers in units of 0.01 km/h per LSB.
requirements.rationale: CAN FD interface is the standard vehicle network bus for this platform. 10 ms cycle time is consistent with existing sensor ECU design.

requirements.title: Maximum operational speed limit
requirements.text: The vehicle speed control system SHALL NOT engage or maintain speed control at vehicle speeds exceeding 180 km/h. If the target speed is set above 180 km/h, the system SHALL clamp the effective target to 180 km/h.
requirements.rationale: 180 km/h is the platform-level maximum design speed for all active safety functions, as defined in vehicle-level system specification SYS-PLAT-007.

requirements.title: Deactivation response time
requirements.text: The vehicle speed control system SHALL fully release all throttle and actuator commands within 100 ms of receiving a deactivation trigger from any source (driver cancel, brake input, clutch input, or fault detection).
requirements.rationale: Derived from HARA HA-VSC-001: unintended acceleration due to delayed deactivation. 100 ms is the maximum tolerable actuator release time from ASIL-B hazard analysis.

requirements.title: OTA software update integrity verification
requirements.text: The vehicle speed control ECU SHALL verify the cryptographic signature of any software update package prior to installation. Updates with invalid or missing signatures SHALL be rejected and the current software version SHALL be retained.
requirements.rationale: Unsigned or tampered software updates are a primary attack vector for ECU compromise (TARA-VSC asset: ECU firmware integrity, threat: malicious software injection, risk level: HIGH). Required by ISO/SAE 21434 cybersecurity goal CG-VSC-002.

requirements.title: Speed sensor plausibility monitoring
requirements.text: The vehicle speed control system SHALL monitor wheel speed sensor inputs for plausibility. A sensor reading SHALL be declared implausible if it deviates by more than 15 km/h from the median of all available wheel speed sensors for more than 200 ms. On implausibility detection, the system SHALL deactivate speed control and set a DTC.
requirements.rationale: Implausible speed sensor data can cause the speed controller to apply incorrect actuator commands. Monitoring is a safety measure for ASIL-B requirement SYS-VSC-001. Thresholds derived from sensor characterization report SENS-VSC-CHR-001.

requirements.title: Driver activation and target speed setting
requirements.text: The vehicle speed control system SHALL allow the driver to activate speed control and set a target speed via the steering wheel controls. The system SHALL only accept activation at vehicle speeds between 30 km/h and 180 km/h.
requirements.rationale: Activation below 30 km/h is not meaningful for highway cruise control. Upper limit consistent with SYS-VSC-004.

requirements.title: Fault notification to driver
requirements.text: The vehicle speed control system SHALL notify the driver via instrument cluster warning lamp and chime within 500 ms of any detected system fault that results in automatic deactivation. The notification SHALL remain active until the driver acknowledges it.
requirements.rationale: Driver awareness of system deactivation is required so the driver resumes manual speed control. Warning timing derived from HARA HA-VSC-002.

requirements.title: Power mode dependency
requirements.text: The vehicle speed control system SHALL only be operational in vehicle power mode RUN. The system SHALL deactivate and retain deactivated state across a power cycle if a fault DTC is set at time of power-down.
requirements.rationale: Speed control must not resume inadvertently after a power cycle following a fault. Consistent with vehicle-level power management specification SYS-PLAT-012.
